danger_signals.visual_form: Color-coded unit sprites (Empire forces vs. Light forces vs. Demon forces)
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies hostile entities on tactical map; signals immediate combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Demonic unit designs and dark aura visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates deception and hidden threat; Empire's reliance on Böser signals unstable alliance

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual equipment status and narrative cutscenes showing sword possession
danger_signals.meaning: Langrisser or Alhazard held by enemy = strategic danger; unclaimed sword = objective hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: Light-themed unit sprites and environmental coloring in controlled territories
safety_signals.meaning: Initial safe faction; Jessica's leadership signals established legitimate authority

safety_signals.visual_form: Named character sprites displayed on map and in party UI
safety_signals.meaning: Friendly unit presence; indicates secured position and reinforcement availability

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: When opposing Erwin on alternate paths, Light forces retain same sprites but function as antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temples and sanctuaries controlled by Light forces; Lushiris' domain

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue color scheme (Blue Dragon Knights under Leon)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Empire units visually distinct from Light units; demonic corruption visual when allied with Böser
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imperial fortifications and military installations; Emperor Bernhardt's seat of power

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic imagery; Böser's dark lord aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demonic unit designs with corruption effects; Böser's personal forces visually distinct
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dark domains; Alhazard's sealed location; areas of magical corruption

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Erwin's forces visually separate from all three original factions once independent
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Neutral territories; Lushiris' domain invaded by Independent forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Light faction colors dominate conquered territories; religious iconography increases; Lushiris does not appear (already aligned)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Erwin complies with goddess will; Empire forces progressively weakened; Bernhardt increases reliance on Böser (visual corruption of Empire forces increases)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue Dragon aesthetic spreads across map; Imperial fortifications strengthen; Alhazard becomes visible symbol of power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jessica and Light forces become antagonists (visual role reversal); Leon and Erwin become allied; former enemies become allies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demonic corruption spreads; dark aura visuals intensify; Alhazard displayed as active threat; world takes on darker color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Erwin assumes sociopathic character; Böser commands Erwin's forces; destruction-focused faction behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Langrisser becomes dominant visual symbol; neutral color scheme; Lushiris appears as active opposing force; world state shows three-faction stalemate breaking
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Erwin opposes Goddess Lushiris directly; Jessica acts as Lushiris' avatar against Erwin; Erwin invades divine domain; peace-focused but defiant of divine will

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kaiser Bernhardt shown with increasing stress/desperation in cutscenes; Böser's demonic forces integrated into Empire visuals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bernhardt attempts suicide as defiance on Light Path; increased reliance on dark magic; moral corruption of Empire

environmental_storytelling.example: Controlled territories shift color based on faction control (Light white → Empire blue → Demon purple), communicating political control without text
Alhazard and Langrisser location on map/in cutscenes signals narrative urgency and factional power balance; sword absence = strategic vulnerability
Erwin standing with Leon (Empire) vs. Jessica (Light) vs. Böser (Demon) visually communicates faction allegiance changes without exposition
Lushiris' domain appearance in late-game signals shift from mortal politics to theological conflict; only appears when Erwin's path opposes her will
Empire forces show progressive dark aura when allied with Böser, signaling moral/magical corruption of the faction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Increases Böser dependency; becomes progressively corrupted; commits suicide as defiance
Rewards Erwin with Alhazard; trusts Erwin with power; maintains stable authority
Attempts ambush of Erwin; flees when ambush fails; reflects on ambitions before death; offers final warnings
Suggests Erwin switch allegiance; becomes ally; frequently obstacles on Light Path but cooperative on others
Leads faction; committed to stopping Empire; allies with Erwin
Becomes antagonist; on Independent path, serves as Lushiris' formal avatar against Erwin; attempts to deactivate Langrisser
Hidden threat; secretly responsible for conflicts; appears to serve Empire but maintains independence
Betrays Empire; places forces under Erwin's command; then can be betrayed by Erwin or sustained as Chaos patron
Does not appear (Erwin complies with will); passive acceptance of outcomes
Becomes active antagonist; opposes Erwin's use of Langrisser; determines Erwin has strayed from guidance; tests humanity's potential

notes: Der Langrisser is notable as one of the first tactical RPGs to implement meaningful faction choice with consequential visual and narrative changes. The game communicates world-state primarily through: (1) faction color-coding on tactical map, (2) character sprite positioning/alignment in cutscenes, (3) legend sword location/possession status, (4) environmental color palette shifts, and (5) divine domain manifestation only when narratively relevant. The political situation is communicated through ally/enemy status reversals rather than explicit text, requiring players to recognize visual faction indicators. Behavioral tells from key NPCs (especially Bernhardt and Jessica) shift dramatically based on player path choice, with same character sprites functioning entirely differently as allies vs. antagonists.
